unit.name: Long-term temporal structure with RNNs and recurrent convolution
unit.description: Explains how RNN/LSTM-style models can aggregate frame or clip features over long videos and how recurrence can be combined with convolution.
unit.summary: The instructor distinguishes short-term clip modeling from long-term temporal structure, where the model must recognize relationships across distant times in long videos [ts=2722s] [ts=2739s]. A practical strategy is to extract local features with a single-frame CNN or 3D CNN and feed the feature sequence into RNNs or LSTMs, producing either a many-to-one video-level prediction at the final timestep or one prediction per frame [ts=2773s] [ts=2794s] [ts=2800s] [ts=2821s]. Training can be difficult end-to-end because the recurrent and convolutional parts form a huge network, so CNNs may be pretrained or frozen and used as feature extractors before training the recurrent module [ts=2855s] [ts=2882s]. The lecture then introduces recurrent convolutional networks: each feature map at a layer/time depends both on the same layer at the previous timestep and the previous layer at the current timestep, making a grid over time and depth [ts=2908s] [ts=2960s]. Instead of RNN matrix multiplications, recurrent convolution replaces those operations with 2D convolutions over feature maps, adds the transformed inputs, and applies tanh; the same idea can be adapted to GRUs and LSTMs [ts=3000s] [ts=3043s] [ts=3062s]. The major limitation is that RNNs are slow and hard to parallelize over long video sequences [ts=3082s] [ts=3095s].

key_points.text: Long videos require modeling temporal structure beyond the short clips handled by earlier models.
Frame or clip features from CNNs can be fed into RNNs or LSTMs to model long-term temporal structure.
RNN video models can be many-to-one for video-level classification or one-to-one for per-frame predictions.
For recurrent video models, CNNs are often pretrained or frozen as feature extractors because full end-to-end training is hard.
A recurrent convolutional feature map depends on the same layer at the previous timestep and the previous layer at the same timestep.
Recurrent convolution replaces RNN matrix multiplications with 2D convolutions over feature maps.
RNN-based video processing is slow and difficult to parallelize for long sequences.

kp_catalog.name: Long-term temporal modeling with RNNs/LSTMs
kp_catalog.description: Frame or clip features can be fed to recurrent models to capture long-range dependencies and produce video-level or frame-level outputs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because long-term temporal modeling with RNNs/LSTMs clarifies frame or clip features can be fed to recurrent models to capture long-range dependencies and produce video-level or frame-level outputs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent convolutional networks
kp_catalog.description: Recurrent convolutional networks combine recurrence with spatial feature maps by replacing recurrent matrix operations with convolutions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent convolutional networks clarifies recurrent convolutional networks combine recurrence with spatial feature maps by replacing recurrent matrix operations with convolutions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
